contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 90
- Description: Liz shares about how we are going to finance the planting of 4,0000 trees this year with the team in Kosovo 🤩🙏
  
  @⁨Kaon⁩ this is the documentation to have your LLM code link against (_not sure which one you are using. Not aware that Gemini has any tool in the Vibe Coding space yet_)
  
  https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_protocol/blob/main/INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md#credentialing--identity
  
  *Followup action items per our conversation*
  
  1. @⁨Kaon⁩ : extend MindLoop to surface in real life experiential learning activity component besides just facts based learning modules
  2. ⁠@⁨Kaon⁩ after user completes a module, to make a call to the credentialing end point to tokenize their credentials with the full set of meta data
  3. ⁠@⁨Kaon⁩ social profile of users that designed and published a experiential learning module
  4. ⁠@⁨Kaon⁩ listing of all experiential learning modules that has been published
  
  5. ⁠@⁨Liz Wong - Shen Zhen Fireworks Dealer⁩ AORA engine needs to expose an API end point which allows listing of available SWAGs or physical artifacts to be bought to facilitate the experiental learning events
  6. ⁠⁠@⁨Liz Wong - Shen Zhen Fireworks Dealer⁩ AORA engine needs to expose an API end point which allows @⁨Kaon⁩ engine to record a newly artifact blueprint when users undergo an experiential learning experience themed around industrial design thinking
  
  _FOR LATER NOT FOR THIS PHASE_
  - ⁠@⁨Liz Wong - Shen Zhen Fireworks Dealer⁩  AORA engine needs to make it easy for Manufacturers to integrate into to system pull the materials they need for their manufacturing activities. We can chat about that later when we meet in China
  
  *Working end to end user experience*
  
  - User undergoes a capoeira experience learning experience: https://capoeira.agroverse.shop/
  - ⁠Experiential learning listing: https://truesight.me/programs/tribomirim/index.html
  - ⁠my on-chain credentials for having undertake a capoeira training modules: https://truesight.me/programs/tribomirim/credentials/#gary-teh
  
  _GUIDING PHILOSOPHY_
  
  From a philosophical stand point, it is important we always put democratization front and end center in our user experience and architectural design point of view as surfaced by @⁨DJ - Kosovo⁩
  
  Under this lens, we must avoid having all these experiences sitting under the MindLoop domain as it will end up creating yet another centralized behemoth like Facebook, Instagram or those hyper-scalers. Big tech is causing so much suffering right now with their hyper consolidation. Even though the one I help built was in the Education Technology vertical, I helped contributed to this trend too. So this is a form of atonement from my prior activities
  
  From an implementation perspective this means that an experiential learning modules when published should allow users the ability to push it as a stand alone set of static HTML/Javascript which can still deliver value to users even if they don’t even use the credentialing or the lesson design module again. 
  
  Github pages allows for each deployment of standalone static html javascript pages. 
  
  https://capoeira.agroverse.shop/ was built with this architectural design philosophy in mind.
  
  Capoeira is a martial arts originated from the African slaves who fled from their colonial masters and escaped into the Kilombo of Bahia Brazil. This martial arts was practiced with the intention of keep the people free from their slave overlords
  
  Avoiding the buildup of a mega data silo will curtail the opportunity for state actors to mess around with the individuals experience. 
  
  NetDragon after acquiring the education platform mishandled the trust that the 100 million users placed on the company. It got it into trouble with the Department of Justice of USA. 
  
  https://www.justice.gov/archives/opa/pr/permanent-injunction-imposed-online-education-platform-company-edmodo-llc-alleged-violations
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh, Elizabeth Wong
- TDG Issued: 150.00
